title: Flight Test Engineer - Aircraft Development Program
setting: An aircraft manufacturer or modifier running performance, handling, and certification flight tests
companySize: Flight-test team of 12-30 inside a program of several hundred engineers
description: You sit at the point where design assumptions meet real aircraft behaviour. Your days blend planning, telemetry review, pilot coordination, data reduction, and risk management. The work is operational, technical, and unforgiving of sloppy preparation.

schedule.activity: Arrive for weather, aircraft status, and telemetry briefing
schedule.note: The morning call decides whether the planned points are still worth flying.

schedule.activity: Run through the test cards with pilots, telemetry staff, and safety personnel
schedule.note: Every test point gets reviewed against abort criteria and expected aircraft response.

schedule.activity: Monitor the sortie and compare live data against predictions
schedule.note: You watch for trends that suggest the envelope expansion should pause.
Pilot comments and quick-look data both matter before the next sortie is authorised.

schedule.activity: Reduce flight data and update the performance or handling assessment
schedule.note: This is where a test point becomes evidence instead of memory.

schedule.activity: Rewrite cards and recommend the next envelope increment
schedule.note: The afternoon often changes tomorrow's flight plan completely.

responsibilities: Coordinate pilots, telemetry, and safety roles
Recommend whether the envelope can expand
challenges: Operational delays from weather, aircraft serviceability, or instrumentation issues
Making sound decisions with incomplete real-time information
Balancing test ambition against safety and evidence quality
rewards: Seeing design predictions tested on a real aircraft
Working close to pilots and the aircraft itself
Playing a visible role in major certification or development milestones
careerPath: Graduate flight-test analyst -> Flight test engineer -> Senior flight test engineer -> Test team lead -> Chief test engineer
